 {"id":"bd-18mg","title":"WebSocket server/acceptor: upgrade handling and connection management","description":"# WebSocket Server/Acceptor Implementation

## Overview
Implements the WebSocket server/acceptor for inbound connections with full Cx integration,
structured concurrency, and cancel-correct client lifecycle management.

## Background & Motivation
Server-side WebSocket is essential for real-time applications: chat, notifications,
live dashboards, multiplayer games. Must handle many concurrent connections with
proper resource management and cancellation.

## Key Design Points

### WebSocket Acceptor
```rust
pub struct WebSocketAcceptor {
    config: WebSocketConfig,
}

impl WebSocketAcceptor {
    /// Accept a WebSocket upgrade from an HTTP request.
    /// The connection is owned by the region that calls accept().
    pub async fn accept(
        &self,
        cx: &Cx,
        req: &Request,
        stream: TcpStream,
    ) -> Outcome<WebSocket, WsError> {
        // 1. Validate upgrade headers (Connection: Upgrade, Upgrade: websocket)
        // 2. Validate Sec-WebSocket-Key
        // 3. Compute Sec-WebSocket-Accept
        // 4. Send 101 Switching Protocols response
        // 5. Return WebSocket in connected state
    }
}
```

### Connection Lifecycle
- Each accepted connection is owned by its accepting region
- Region cancellation propagates to all child WebSocket connections
- Connections send Close frame (1001) when region closes
- Server can broadcast close to all connections on shutdown

### Integration with HTTP Server
```rust
#[route(GET, "/ws")]
async fn websocket_handler(cx: &Cx, req: Request, stream: TcpStream) -> Response {
    let acceptor = WebSocketAcceptor::new(config);
    let ws = acceptor.accept(cx, &req, stream).await?;
    
    // Spawn WebSocket handler in current region
    cx.spawn(async move |cx| {
        while let Ok(msg) = ws.recv(&cx).await {
            // Handle message
        }
    });
    
    // Return upgrade response (already sent by acceptor)
    Response::switching_protocols()
}
```

### Graceful Shutdown
- Server signals shutdown to all connections via Cx cancellation
- Connections attempt Close handshake with timeout
- After timeout, forcibly drop connections
- No message loss for pending sends

## Dependencies
- Requires: WebSocket frame codec (bd-r4hu)
- Requires: WebSocket handshake (bd-1kg0)
- Blocks: WebSocket E2E tests

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] accept() validates and completes WebSocket handshake
- [ ] Connections owned by accepting region
- [ ] Cancellation triggers clean close with 1001
- [ ] Integration with HTTP server layer
- [ ] Subprotocol negotiation support
- [ ] Extension negotiation (permessage-deflate)
- [ ] Unit tests for accept/reject paths
- [ ] Integration tests with real HTTP server","notes":"## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
- `ws_server::tests::accept_valid_upgrade` - Accept valid WebSocket upgrade
- `ws_server::tests::reject_missing_upgrade_header` - Reject without Upgrade header
- `ws_server::tests::reject_invalid_key` - Reject invalid Sec-WebSocket-Key
- `ws_server::tests::compute_accept_key` - Verify Sec-WebSocket-Accept computation
- `ws_server::tests::subprotocol_negotiation` - Select subprotocol
- `ws_server::tests::extension_negotiation` - Handle extension negotiation
- `ws_server::tests::send_recv_messages` - Basic message exchange
- `ws_server::tests::close_initiated_by_server` - Server initiates close
- `ws_server::tests::close_initiated_by_client` - Handle client close

### Cancel-Correctness Tests
- `ws_server::cancel::cancel_during_accept` - Cancel during handshake
- `ws_server::cancel::cancel_active_connection` - Cancel with active connection
- `ws_server::cancel::region_close_all_connections` - Mass close on region close
- `ws_server::cancel::graceful_shutdown` - Ordered shutdown with timeout

### Integration Tests
- `ws_server::integration::accept_multiple_clients` - Multiple concurrent connections
- `ws_server::integration::broadcast_message` - Send to all clients
- `ws_server::integration::client_disconnect_handling` - Handle client drops
- `ws_server::integration::http_server_integration` - With HTTP layer

### Logging Requirements
- TRACE: Frame details, handshake headers
- DEBUG: Accept/reject decisions, message flow
- INFO: Connection lifecycle (accept, close)
- WARN: Invalid upgrade attempts, protocol violations
- ERROR: Accept failures with full context

### Test Script
```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
export RUST_LOG="asupersync::ws::server=debug,test=info"
cargo test -p asupersync ws_server:: -- --nocapture 2>&1 | tee ws_server_tests.log
```","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T01:16:58.167942145Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T08:13:34.791662855Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T08:13:34.791560485Z","close_reason":"Implemented WebSocket server/acceptor with Cx integration: WebSocketAcceptor for upgrade validation, ServerWebSocket for server-side connections, accept()/reject() methods, protocol/extension negotiation. Tests blocked by scheduler errors.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ecosystem-parity","websocket"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-18mg","depends_on_id":"bd-1kg0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-18mg","depends_on_id":"bd-ku6n","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-18mg","depends_on_id":"bd-r4hu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:05Z","created_by":"import"}]}

- Output must be stable (prefer artifacts checked by hashes rather than timing).

 {"id":"bd-1ikk","title":"[ALIEN][SCHED][LYAP] Spec: scheduler policy seam + determinism rules","description":"## Purpose

Define exactly how the Lyapunov governor is allowed to influence scheduling without breaking correctness or determinism.

This is the "policy object" seam: the scheduler remains correct by construction; the governor only affects deterministic tie-breaking and queue selection within allowed constraints.

## Conceptual model

We maintain a potential function over the runtime state Sigma:

V(Sigma) = w1 * obligations_unresolved
         + w2 * cancel_debt
         + w3 * deadline_slack_penalty
         + w4 * region_close_pressure
         + ...

The governor proposes actions that maximize drift reduction (-Delta V), subject to hard invariants.

In the deterministic lab runtime, "expected" is implemented as deterministic evaluation of candidate actions using the same snapshot function; no sampling.

## Allowed influence surface (must be explicit)

- tie-breaking among runnable tasks within the same lane
- selection among multiple ready queues when semantics permits
- optional: promotion within bounds (e.g., run cancel-debt tasks earlier)

## Hard invariants (non-negotiable)

- cancel lane strictness unless formally justified (cancel protocol must not be delayed)
- determinism: no wall-clock, no ambient RNG
- bounded fairness: governor cannot starve a runnable task indefinitely
- no semantic changes: only reordering of runnable work, not skipping required protocol steps

## Evidence ledger (galaxy-brain mode)

When enabled for debugging (trace-backed, bounded):
- record V decomposition for each decision
- record candidate comparisons: why task X beat task Y (Delta V components)
- record any policy constraints that forced a suboptimal choice

This must be emitted via tracing/trace events, not stdout.

## Acceptance criteria

- A clear policy interface exists (trait or config) specifying inputs/outputs and allowed hooks.
- Explicit deterministic ordering/tie-break rules are specified (including stable iteration order for candidate sets).
- A fairness story exists (e.g., governor score + age-based tiebreak, or bounded round-robin within score bands).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:12:07.860973918Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T02:52:38.343508060Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T02:52:38.343409066Z","close_reason":"Added scheduler policy seam + determinism rules to asupersync_plan_v4.md","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien","cancellation","scheduler"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ikk","depends_on_id":"bd-12rg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:22:51Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1jis","title":"Logical clocks: happens-before ordering for distributed events","description":"# Logical Clocks Implementation

## Goal

Implement logical clocks for ordering events in the distributed runtime, replacing wall-clock time with causally consistent ordering.

## Background

Wall clocks are unreliable in distributed systems:
- Clock skew between machines
- NTP adjustments can jump backwards
- No global 'now' across machines

Logical clocks provide:
- Happens-before ordering (causal consistency)
- Partial order on events
- Sufficient for most coordination

## Clock Types

### Lamport Clocks
Simplest logical clock:
```rust
pub struct LamportClock {
    counter: AtomicU64,
}

impl LamportClock {
    /// Tick for local event
    fn tick(&self) -> LamportTime {
        LamportTime(self.counter.fetch_add(1, Ordering::SeqCst))
    }
    
    /// Update on message receive
    fn receive(&self, sender_time: LamportTime) -> LamportTime {
        let local = self.counter.load(Ordering::SeqCst);
        let new = std::cmp::max(local, sender_time.0) + 1;
        self.counter.store(new, Ordering::SeqCst);
        LamportTime(new)
    }
}

#[derive(Clone, Copy, Ord, PartialOrd, Eq, PartialEq)]
pub struct LamportTime(u64);
```

Properties:
- If a → b (a happens before b), then L(a) < L(b)
- But L(a) < L(b) does NOT imply a → b (concurrent events)

### Vector Clocks
Full causal ordering:
```rust
pub struct VectorClock {
    /// One counter per node
    counters: HashMap<NodeId, u64>,
    
    /// This node's ID
    self_id: NodeId,
}

impl VectorClock {
    /// Tick for local event
    fn tick(&mut self) -> VectorTime {
        *self.counters.entry(self.self_id).or_insert(0) += 1;
        VectorTime(self.counters.clone())
    }
    
    /// Update on message receive
    fn receive(&mut self, sender_time: &VectorTime) {
        for (node, &count) in &sender_time.0 {
            let entry = self.counters.entry(*node).or_insert(0);
            *entry = std::cmp::max(*entry, count);
        }
        self.tick();
    }
    
    /// Check if this happened before other
    fn happened_before(&self, other: &VectorTime) -> bool {
        // self ≤ other and self ≠ other
        self.counters.iter().all(|(k, &v)| {
            other.0.get(k).map_or(v == 0, |&ov| v <= ov)
        }) && self.counters != other.0
    }
}

#[derive(Clone, Eq, PartialEq)]
pub struct VectorTime(HashMap<NodeId, u64>);
```

Properties:
- V(a) < V(b) IFF a → b
- Neither < means concurrent

### Hybrid Logical Clocks
Best of both worlds:
```rust
pub struct HybridClock {
    /// Logical component
    logical: AtomicU64,
    
    /// Physical component (bounded wall clock)
    physical: AtomicU64,
    
    /// Maximum tolerated skew
    max_skew: Duration,
}

impl HybridClock {
    fn tick(&self) -> HybridTime {
        let physical = wall_clock_ms();
        let logical = self.logical.load(Ordering::SeqCst);
        
        let new_physical = std::cmp::max(physical, self.physical.load(Ordering::SeqCst));
        
        if new_physical == self.physical.load(Ordering::SeqCst) {
            self.logical.fetch_add(1, Ordering::SeqCst);
        } else {
            self.physical.store(new_physical, Ordering::SeqCst);
            self.logical.store(0, Ordering::SeqCst);
        }
        
        HybridTime { physical: new_physical, logical: self.logical.load(Ordering::SeqCst) }
    }
}

#[derive(Clone, Copy, Ord, PartialOrd, Eq, PartialEq)]
pub struct HybridTime {
    physical: u64,  // Milliseconds since epoch
    logical: u64,   // Tiebreaker within same millisecond
}
```

Properties:
- Total order (unlike vector clocks)
- Bounded skew from real time
- Used by CockroachDB, YugabyteDB

## LogicalClock Trait

```rust
pub trait LogicalClock: Send + Sync {
    type Time: Clone + Ord + Send;
    
    /// Record a local event
    fn tick(&self) -> Self::Time;
    
    /// Update based on received message
    fn receive(&self, sender_time: &Self::Time) -> Self::Time;
    
    /// Get current time without ticking
    fn now(&self) -> Self::Time;
}
```

## Integration with Cx

```rust
impl Cx {
    /// Get logical timestamp
    fn logical_now(&self) -> LogicalTime;
    
    /// Tick for local event
    fn logical_tick(&self) -> LogicalTime;
    
    /// Update from message
    fn logical_receive(&self, sender_time: LogicalTime);
}
```

## Wire Protocol Integration

Every network message includes sender's logical time:
```rust
struct MessageEnvelope<M> {
    sender: NodeId,
    sender_time: LogicalTime,
    payload: M,
}
```

Receiver updates clock on every message.

## Lab Runtime

Lab runtime uses simplified logical clock:
- Deterministic counter (no wall clock)
- Same seed = same clock progression
- Virtual time mapped to logical time

## Choosing a Clock

| Clock | Size | Ordering | Use Case |
|-------|------|----------|----------|
| Lamport | O(1) | Partial | Simple coordination |
| Vector | O(nodes) | Total causal | Debug, conflict detection |
| Hybrid | O(1) | Total | Production systems |

Default: Hybrid for production, Lamport for lab testing.

## Overview
Async DNS resolver with caching, connection reuse, and Happy Eyeballs (RFC 6555) support.

## Why This Is Critical
DNS resolution is required for:
- HTTP client connections
- Any client-initiated network connection
- Service discovery

The standard library's DNS resolution is blocking; we need async resolution for proper integration.

## Core Types

### Resolver
```rust
/// Async DNS resolver.
pub struct Resolver {
    config: ResolverConfig,
    cache: DnsCache,
    // Connection pool for DNS servers
    pool: ConnectionPool,
}

impl Resolver {
    /// Create resolver with system configuration.
    pub async fn from_system_conf() -> Result<Self, DnsError>;

    /// Create resolver with custom configuration.
    pub fn new(config: ResolverConfig) -> Self;

    /// Lookup IP addresses for a hostname.
    pub async fn lookup_ip(&self, host: &str) -> Result<LookupIp, DnsError>;

    /// Lookup with Happy Eyeballs (RFC 6555).
    /// Returns addresses interleaved IPv6/IPv4 for optimal connection racing.
    pub async fn lookup_ip_happy(&self, host: &str) -> Result<HappyEyeballs, DnsError>;

    /// Lookup MX records.
    pub async fn lookup_mx(&self, domain: &str) -> Result<LookupMx, DnsError>;

    /// Lookup TXT records.

    /// Lookup SRV records.
    pub async fn lookup_srv(&self, name: &str) -> Result<LookupSrv, DnsError>;

    /// Reverse lookup (PTR record).
    pub async fn reverse_lookup(&self, addr: IpAddr) -> Result<LookupPtr, DnsError>;

    /// Clear the cache.
    pub fn clear_cache(&self);
}
```

 {"id":"bd-wbz","title":"Implement futurelock detector (lab/debug)","description":"# Futurelock Detector (lab/debug)

## Purpose
Detect and surface the design-rule violation:

> Never allow a primitive to stop being polled while holding an obligation without transferring it, aborting/nacking it, or escalating.

In practice this shows up as a *futurelock*: a task holds one or more unresolved obligations (permits/acks/leases/IoOps) but is no longer being polled (e.g. it awaited something that never wakes, or it was dropped/forgotten by a buggy primitive).

Phase 0 needs this in **lab/debug** mode so we can turn “subtle deadlocks/leaks” into deterministic test failures with actionable evidence.

## Spec Background
- Design Bible §8.6: “Futurelock detector”
- Operational Semantics §1.9 + §3.4: obligations are linear; leaks are semantic errors
- Key invariant: a region cannot close while obligations in the region remain `Reserved`

A futurelock is *strictly worse* than a normal leak:
- a leak is “task completed while holding obligation”
- a futurelock is “task *didn’t* complete, but is also no longer being polled, so it can’t resolve the obligation”

## Design (Plan-of-Record)
### Observable behavior
When enabled, the lab runtime MUST:
1. Detect tasks that hold ≥1 `Reserved` obligations and have not been polled for a bounded number of lab steps (configurable).
2. Emit a trace-visible event with enough context to debug.
3. Optionally fail-fast (panic) in lab mode when the threshold is exceeded.

### Data we need
- `last_polled_step: u64` per task (updated every time we poll the task).
- Ability to query “held obligations” per task (from the obligation registry).
- Global `step_counter: u64` in the lab runtime.

### Detection rule
On each lab step (or at least whenever the scheduler makes progress):
- For each task `t` that currently holds ≥1 `Reserved` obligation:
  - if `step_counter - last_polled_step(t) > futurelock_max_idle_steps` then `futurelock_detected(t, …)`.

We should explicitly ignore tasks that are already terminal.

### Trace model
Add a semantic trace event (names flexible):
- `TraceEvent::FuturelockDetected { task: TaskId, region: RegionId, idle_steps: u64, held: Vec<ObligationId>, kinds: Vec<ObligationKind> }`

Constraints:
- The trace event must be deterministic and stable.
- Do NOT allocate on the hot path in production; this is lab/debug functionality.

### Config knobs
Add lab config fields (names flexible):
- `futurelock_max_idle_steps: u64` (default: conservative but small, e.g. 10_000)
- `panic_on_futurelock: bool` (default: true in lab tests)

## Testing
### Unit tests
- Construct a tiny obligation registry state where a task holds a reserved obligation and has an old `last_polled_step`; assert detection triggers.

### E2E lab scenarios
- Scenario: task reserves a SendPermit and then awaits a future that never wakes; another task continues making progress so the lab runtime keeps stepping. Assert:
  - a FuturelockDetected trace event appears
  - (if enabled) the runtime panics with a message that includes task id + obligation ids

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deterministic detection in lab mode (same seed/config => same detection point and trace).
- Trace event contains enough context to debug (task, region, obligation ids/kinds, idle steps).
- Does not introduce stdout/stderr logging in core runtime (only trace + test harness output).
- Clear documentation in the issue text about what counts as a futurelock and what does not.

 {"id":"bd-wcmz","title":"[Stream] Implement Collection and Terminal Operations","description":"# Collection and Terminal Operations

## Overview
Terminal operations that consume streams and produce final results.

## Implementation Steps

### Step 1: Collect
```rust
/// Collect stream into a collection
pub struct Collect<S, C> {
    stream: S,
    collection: C,
}

impl<S, C> Future for Collect<S, C>
where
    S: Stream,
    C: Default + Extend<S::Item>,
{
    type Output = C;
    
    fn poll(self: Pin<&mut Self>, cx: &mut Context<'_>) -> Poll<C> {
        let this = unsafe { self.get_unchecked_mut() };
        
        loop {
            match Pin::new(&mut this.stream).poll_next(cx) {
                Poll::Ready(Some(item)) => {
                    this.collection.extend(std::iter::once(item));
                }
                Poll::Ready(None) => {
                    return Poll::Ready(std::mem::take(&mut this.collection));
                }
                Poll::Pending => return Poll::Pending,
            }
        }
    }
}

// Extension method
fn collect<C: Default + Extend<Self::Item>>(self) -> Collect<Self, C>
where
    Self: Sized,
{
    Collect {
        stream: self,
        collection: C::default(),
    }
}
```

### Step 2: Fold
```rust
/// Fold stream into single value
pub struct Fold<S, F, Acc> {
    stream: S,
    f: F,
    acc: Option<Acc>,
}

impl<S, F, Acc> Future for Fold<S, F, Acc>
where
    S: Stream,
    F: FnMut(Acc, S::Item) -> Acc,
{
    type Output = Acc;
    
    fn poll(self: Pin<&mut Self>, cx: &mut Context<'_>) -> Poll<Acc> {
        let this = unsafe { self.get_unchecked_mut() };
        
        loop {
            match Pin::new(&mut this.stream).poll_next(cx) {
                Poll::Ready(Some(item)) => {
                    let acc = this.acc.take().unwrap();
                    this.acc = Some((this.f)(acc, item));
                }
                Poll::Ready(None) => {
                    return Poll::Ready(this.acc.take().unwrap());
                }
                Poll::Pending => return Poll::Pending,
            }
        }
    }
}

// Extension method
fn fold<Acc, F>(self, init: Acc, f: F) -> Fold<Self, F, Acc>
where
    Self: Sized,
    F: FnMut(Acc, Self::Item) -> Acc,
{
    Fold {
        stream: self,
        f,
        acc: Some(init),
    }
}
```

### Step 3: ForEach
```rust
/// Execute function for each item
pub struct ForEach<S, F> {
    stream: S,
    f: F,
}

impl<S, F> Future for ForEach<S, F>
where
    S: Stream,
    F: FnMut(S::Item),
{
    type Output = ();
    
    fn poll(self: Pin<&mut Self>, cx: &mut Context<'_>) -> Poll<()> {
        let this = unsafe { self.get_unchecked_mut() };
        
        loop {
            match Pin::new(&mut this.stream).poll_next(cx) {
                Poll::Ready(Some(item)) => {
                    (this.f)(item);
                }
                Poll::Ready(None) => return Poll::Ready(()),
                Poll::Pending => return Poll::Pending,
            }
        }
    }
}

// Async variant
pub struct ForEachAsync<S, F, Fut> {
    stream: S,
    f: F,
    pending: Option<Fut>,
}

impl<S, F, Fut> Future for ForEachAsync<S, F, Fut>
where
    S: Stream,
    F: FnMut(S::Item) -> Fut,
    Fut: Future<Output = ()>,
{
    type Output = ();
    
    fn poll(self: Pin<&mut Self>, cx: &mut Context<'_>) -> Poll<()> {
        let this = unsafe { self.get_unchecked_mut() };
        
        loop {
            // Complete pending future first
            if let Some(fut) = &mut this.pending {
                match Pin::new(fut).poll(cx) {
                    Poll::Ready(()) => {
                        this.pending = None;
                    }
                    Poll::Pending => return Poll::Pending,
                }
            }
            
            // Get next item
            match Pin::new(&mut this.stream).poll_next(cx) {
                Poll::Ready(Some(item)) => {
                    this.pending = Some((this.f)(item));
                }
                Poll::Ready(None) => return Poll::Ready(()),
                Poll::Pending => return Poll::Pending,
            }
        }
    }
}

/// Count items in stream
pub struct Count<S> {
    stream: S,
    count: usize,
}

impl<S: Stream> Future for Count<S> {
    type Output = usize;
    
    fn poll(self: Pin<&mut Self>, cx: &mut Context<'_>) -> Poll<usize> {
        let this = unsafe { self.get_unchecked_mut() };
        
        loop {
            match Pin::new(&mut this.stream).poll_next(cx) {
                Poll::Ready(Some(_)) => this.count += 1,
                Poll::Ready(None) => return Poll::Ready(this.count),
                Poll::Pending => return Poll::Pending,
            }
        }
    }
}

// Extension methods
fn count(self) -> Count<Self>
where
    Self: Sized,
{
    Count { stream: self, count: 0 }
}

fn sum<S>(self) -> Sum<Self>
where
    Self: Sized + Stream<Item = S>,
    S: std::iter::Sum,
{
    Sum { stream: self }
}

### Step 5: Any, All
```rust
/// Check if any item matches predicate
pub struct Any<S, F> {
    stream: S,
    predicate: F,
}

impl<S, F> Future for Any<S, F>
where
    S: Stream,
    F: FnMut(&S::Item) -> bool,
{
    type Output = bool;
    
    fn poll(self: Pin<&mut Self>, cx: &mut Context<'_>) -> Poll<bool> {
        let this = unsafe { self.get_unchecked_mut() };
        
        loop {
            match Pin::new(&mut this.stream).poll_next(cx) {
                Poll::Ready(Some(item)) => {
                    if (this.predicate)(&item) {
                        return Poll::Ready(true);
                    }
                }
                Poll::Ready(None) => return Poll::Ready(false),
                Poll::Pending => return Poll::Pending,
            }
        }
    }
}

/// Check if all items match predicate
pub struct All<S, F> {
    stream: S,
    predicate: F,
}

impl<S, F> Future for All<S, F>
where
    S: Stream,
    F: FnMut(&S::Item) -> bool,
{
    type Output = bool;
    
    fn poll(self: Pin<&mut Self>, cx: &mut Context<'_>) -> Poll<bool> {
        let this = unsafe { self.get_unchecked_mut() };
        
        loop {
            match Pin::new(&mut this.stream).poll_next(cx) {
                Poll::Ready(Some(item)) => {
                    if \!(this.predicate)(&item) {
                        return Poll::Ready(false);
                    }
                }
                Poll::Ready(None) => return Poll::Ready(true),
                Poll::Pending => return Poll::Pending,
            }
        }
    }
}

### Step 6: TryCollect and TryFold
```rust
/// Collect stream of Results
pub struct TryCollect<S, C> {
    stream: S,
    collection: C,
}

impl<S, T, E, C> Future for TryCollect<S, C>
where
    S: Stream<Item = Result<T, E>>,
    C: Default + Extend<T>,
{
    type Output = Result<C, E>;
    
    fn poll(self: Pin<&mut Self>, cx: &mut Context<'_>) -> Poll<Result<C, E>> {
        let this = unsafe { self.get_unchecked_mut() };
        
        loop {
            match Pin::new(&mut this.stream).poll_next(cx) {
                Poll::Ready(Some(Ok(item))) => {
                    this.collection.extend(std::iter::once(item));
                }
                Poll::Ready(Some(Err(e))) => {
                    return Poll::Ready(Err(e));
                }
                Poll::Ready(None) => {
                    return Poll::Ready(Ok(std::mem::take(&mut this.collection)));
                }
                Poll::Pending => return Poll::Pending,
            }
        }
    }
}

/// Fold stream of Results
pub struct TryFold<S, F, Acc> {
    stream: S,
    f: F,
    acc: Option<Acc>,
}

impl<S, F, Acc, T, E> Future for TryFold<S, F, Acc>
where
    S: Stream<Item = Result<T, E>>,
    F: FnMut(Acc, T) -> Result<Acc, E>,
{
    type Output = Result<Acc, E>;
    
    fn poll(self: Pin<&mut Self>, cx: &mut Context<'_>) -> Poll<Result<Acc, E>> {
        let this = unsafe { self.get_unchecked_mut() };
        
        loop {
            match Pin::new(&mut this.stream).poll_next(cx) {
                Poll::Ready(Some(Ok(item))) => {
                    let acc = this.acc.take().unwrap();
                    match (this.f)(acc, item) {
                        Ok(new_acc) => this.acc = Some(new_acc),
                        Err(e) => return Poll::Ready(Err(e)),
                    }
                }
                Poll::Ready(Some(Err(e))) => return Poll::Ready(Err(e)),
                Poll::Ready(None) => {
                    return Poll::Ready(Ok(this.acc.take().unwrap()));
                }
                Poll::Pending => return Poll::Pending,
            }
        }
    }
}

            sum = stats.1,

### Step 1: Create IntoResponse Trait

```rust
// src/web/response/mod.rs

use crate::http::{Response, StatusCode, HeaderMap, HeaderName, HeaderValue};

/// Trait for types that can be converted into an HTTP response.
pub trait IntoResponse {
    /// Convert self into a Response.
    fn into_response(self) -> Response;
}

// Implement for Response itself
impl IntoResponse for Response {
    fn into_response(self) -> Response {
        self
    }
}

// Implement for ()
impl IntoResponse for () {
    fn into_response(self) -> Response {
        Response::builder()
            .status(StatusCode::OK)
            .body(Vec::new())
            .unwrap()
    }
}

// Implement for String
impl IntoResponse for String {
    fn into_response(self) -> Response {
        Response::builder()
            .status(StatusCode::OK)
            .header("content-type", "text/plain; charset=utf-8")
            .body(self.into_bytes())
            .unwrap()
    }
}

// Implement for &'static str
impl IntoResponse for &'static str {
    fn into_response(self) -> Response {
        self.to_string().into_response()
    }
}

// Implement for Vec<u8>
impl IntoResponse for Vec<u8> {
    fn into_response(self) -> Response {
        Response::builder()
            .status(StatusCode::OK)
            .header("content-type", "application/octet-stream")
            .body(self)
            .unwrap()
    }
}

// Implement for StatusCode alone
impl IntoResponse for StatusCode {
    fn into_response(self) -> Response {
        Response::builder()
            .status(self)
            .body(Vec::new())
            .unwrap()
    }
}

// Implement for tuples (StatusCode, T)
impl<T: IntoResponse> IntoResponse for (StatusCode, T) {
    fn into_response(self) -> Response {
        let (status, body) = self;
        let mut response = body.into_response();
        *response.status_mut() = status;
        response
    }
}

// Implement for tuples (StatusCode, HeaderMap, T)
impl<T: IntoResponse> IntoResponse for (StatusCode, HeaderMap, T) {
    fn into_response(self) -> Response {
        let (status, headers, body) = self;
        let mut response = body.into_response();
        *response.status_mut() = status;
        response.headers_mut().extend(headers);
        response
    }
}

// Implement for Result<T, E>
impl<T: IntoResponse, E: IntoResponse> IntoResponse for Result<T, E> {
    fn into_response(self) -> Response {
        match self {
            Ok(v) => v.into_response(),
            Err(e) => e.into_response(),
        }
    }
}

// Implement for Option<T>
impl<T: IntoResponse> IntoResponse for Option<T> {
    fn into_response(self) -> Response {
        match self {
            Some(v) => v.into_response(),
            None => StatusCode::NOT_FOUND.into_response(),
        }
    }
}
```

### Step 2: Implement JSON Response Type

// src/web/response/json.rs

use serde::Serialize;

/// JSON response wrapper.
///
/// # Example
/// ```rust
/// async fn get_user(Path(id): Path<u32>) -> Json<User> {
///     let user = User { id, name: "Alice".into() };
///     Json(user)
/// }
/// ```
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct Json<T>(pub T);

impl<T: Serialize> IntoResponse for Json<T> {
    fn into_response(self) -> Response {
        match serde_json::to_vec(&self.0) {
            Ok(bytes) => Response::builder()
                .status(StatusCode::OK)
                .header("content-type", "application/json")
                .body(bytes)
                .unwrap(),
            Err(e) => Response::builder()
                .status(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)
                .body(format!("JSON serialization error: {}", e).into_bytes())
                .unwrap(),
        }
    }
}

/// JSON response with custom status code.
impl<T: Serialize> IntoResponse for (StatusCode, Json<T>) {
    fn into_response(self) -> Response {
        let (status, json) = self;
        let mut response = json.into_response();
        *response.status_mut() = status;
        response
    }
}

// src/web/response/redirect.rs

/// HTTP redirect response.
///
/// # Example
/// ```rust
/// async fn old_page() -> Redirect {
///     Redirect::permanent("/new-page")
/// }
/// ```
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct Redirect {
    status: StatusCode,
    location: String,
}

impl Redirect {
    /// Temporary redirect (302).
    pub fn to(uri: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
        Self {
            status: StatusCode::FOUND,
            location: uri.into(),
        }
    }

    /// Permanent redirect (301).
    pub fn permanent(uri: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
        Self {
            status: StatusCode::MOVED_PERMANENTLY,
            location: uri.into(),
        }
    }

    /// See Other redirect (303) - used after POST.
    pub fn see_other(uri: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
        Self {
            status: StatusCode::SEE_OTHER,
            location: uri.into(),
        }
    }

    /// Temporary redirect (307) - preserves method.
    pub fn temporary(uri: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
        Self {
            status: StatusCode::TEMPORARY_REDIRECT,
            location: uri.into(),
        }
    }
}

impl IntoResponse for Redirect {
    fn into_response(self) -> Response {
        Response::builder()
            .status(self.status)
            .header("location", self.location)
            .body(Vec::new())
            .unwrap()
    }
}

```rust
// src/web/response/error.rs

/// Standard HTTP error response.
#[derive(Debug)]
pub struct AppError {
    status: StatusCode,
    message: String,
    details: Option<serde_json::Value>,
}

impl AppError {
    pub fn new(status: StatusCode, message: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
        Self {
            status,
            message: message.into(),
            details: None,
        }
    }

    pub fn bad_request(message: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
        Self::new(StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST, message)
    }

    pub fn unauthorized(message: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
        Self::new(StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED, message)
    }

    pub fn forbidden(message: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
        Self::new(StatusCode::FORBIDDEN, message)
    }

    pub fn not_found(message: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
        Self::new(StatusCode::NOT_FOUND, message)
    }

    pub fn internal(message: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
        Self::new(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, message)
    }

    pub fn with_details(mut self, details: impl Serialize) -> Self {
        self.details = serde_json::to_value(details).ok();
        self
    }
}

impl IntoResponse for AppError {
    fn into_response(self) -> Response {
        let body = serde_json::json!({
            "error": {
                "code": self.status.as_u16(),
                "message": self.message,
                "details": self.details,
            }
        });

        Response::builder()
            .status(self.status)
            .header("content-type", "application/json")
            .body(serde_json::to_vec(&body).unwrap())
            .unwrap()
    }
}

// Implement IntoResponse for common error types
impl IntoResponse for std::io::Error {
    fn into_response(self) -> Response {
        AppError::internal(self.to_string()).into_response()
    }
}

impl IntoResponse for serde_json::Error {
    fn into_response(self) -> Response {
        AppError::bad_request(format!("JSON error: {}", self)).into_response()
    }
}

### Step 6: Implement Response Extensions

```rust
// src/web/response/ext.rs

/// Extension trait for Response building.
pub trait ResponseExt {
    /// Set a cookie on the response.
    fn with_cookie(self, cookie: Cookie) -> Self;

    /// Set cache control headers.
    fn with_cache_control(self, directive: CacheControl) -> Self;

    /// Set CORS headers.
    fn with_cors(self, origin: &str) -> Self;
}

impl ResponseExt for Response {
    fn with_cookie(mut self, cookie: Cookie) -> Self {
        self.headers_mut().append(
            "set-cookie",
            cookie.to_string().parse().unwrap(),
        );
        self
    }

    fn with_cache_control(mut self, directive: CacheControl) -> Self {
        self.headers_mut().insert(
            "cache-control",
            directive.to_string().parse().unwrap(),
        );
        self
    }

    fn with_cors(mut self, origin: &str) -> Self {
        self.headers_mut().insert(
            "access-control-allow-origin",
            origin.parse().unwrap(),
        );
        self
    }
}

/// Cache control directives.
pub enum CacheControl {
    NoCache,
    NoStore,
    Public(Duration),
    Private(Duration),
    MaxAge(Duration),
}

impl std::fmt::Display for CacheControl {
    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
        match self {
            Self::NoCache => write!(f, "no-cache"),
            Self::NoStore => write!(f, "no-store"),
            Self::Public(d) => write!(f, "public, max-age={}", d.as_secs()),
            Self::Private(d) => write!(f, "private, max-age={}", d.as_secs()),
            Self::MaxAge(d) => write!(f, "max-age={}", d.as_secs()),
        }
    }
}

## Cancel-Safety Considerations

- Response building is synchronous - inherently cancel-safe
- JSON serialization happens synchronously before returning
- No async operations in IntoResponse implementations

            #[derive(Serialize)]

            let router = Router::new()
                .get("/text", || async { "Hello, World!" })
                .get("/json", || async {

                })
                .get("/created", || async {
                    (StatusCode::CREATED, Json(User { id: 2, name: "Bob".into() }))
                })
                .get("/redirect", || async {
                    Redirect::to("/new-location")
                })
                .get("/error", || async {
                    Err::<(), _>(AppError::not_found("resource not found"))
                });

            // Test text response
            let req = Request::get("/text").unwrap();
            let resp = router.clone().call(req).await.unwrap();
            assert_eq!(resp.status(), 200);
            assert!(resp.headers().get("content-type").unwrap()
                .to_str().unwrap().contains("text/plain"));

            // Test JSON response
            let req = Request::get("/json").unwrap();
            let resp = router.clone().call(req).await.unwrap();
            assert_eq!(resp.status(), 200);
            assert!(resp.headers().get("content-type").unwrap()
                .to_str().unwrap().contains("application/json"));

            // Test status + JSON
            let req = Request::get("/created").unwrap();
            let resp = router.clone().call(req).await.unwrap();
            assert_eq!(resp.status(), 201);

            // Test redirect
            let req = Request::get("/redirect").unwrap();
            let resp = router.clone().call(req).await.unwrap();
            assert_eq!(resp.status(), 302);
            assert_eq!(resp.headers().get("location").unwrap(), "/new-location");

            // Test error
            let req = Request::get("/error").unwrap();
            let resp = router.call(req).await.unwrap();
            assert_eq!(resp.status(), 404);

            info!("E2E response types test passed");
        });
    }
}

Define the core Pool trait and PooledResource type with obligation-based return semantics.

## Pool Trait

```rust
use std::error::Error;

/// Trait for resource pools with cancel-safe acquisition.
#[async_trait]
pub trait Pool: Send + Sync {
    /// The type of resource managed by this pool.
    type Resource: Send;
    
    /// Error type for acquisition failures.
    type Error: Error + Send + Sync + 'static;
    
    /// Acquire a resource from the pool.
    /// 
    /// This may block if no resources are available and the pool
    /// is at capacity. The acquire respects the Cx's deadline.
    /// 
    /// # Cancel-Safety
    /// 
    /// If cancelled while waiting, no resource is leaked.
    /// If cancelled after acquisition, the PooledResource's
    /// obligation ensures the resource is returned.
    async fn acquire(&self, cx: &Cx) -> Result<PooledResource<Self::Resource>, Self::Error>;
    
    /// Try to acquire without waiting.
    /// 
    /// Returns None if no resource is immediately available.
    fn try_acquire(&self) -> Option<PooledResource<Self::Resource>>;
    
    /// Get current pool statistics.
    fn stats(&self) -> PoolStats;
    
    /// Close the pool, rejecting new acquisitions.
    async fn close(&self);
}
```

## PooledResource

```rust
/// A resource acquired from a pool.
/// 
/// This type uses the obligation system to ensure the resource
/// is returned to the pool, even if the holding task is cancelled.
pub struct PooledResource<R> {
    /// The underlying resource.
    resource: Option<R>,
    
    /// Obligation to return the resource.
    return_obligation: Obligation,
    
    /// Channel to send resource back to pool.
    return_tx: Sender<PoolReturn<R>>,
    
    /// When this resource was acquired.
    acquired_at: Instant,
}

enum PoolReturn<R> {
    /// Resource is healthy, return to idle pool.
    Return(R),
    /// Resource is broken, discard it.
    Discard,
}

impl<R> PooledResource<R> {
    /// Access the resource.
    pub fn get(&self) -> &R {
        self.resource.as_ref().expect("resource taken")
    }
    
    /// Mutably access the resource.
    pub fn get_mut(&mut self) -> &mut R {
        self.resource.as_mut().expect("resource taken")
    }
    
    /// Explicitly return the resource to the pool.
    /// 
    /// This discharges the return obligation.
    pub fn return_to_pool(mut self) {
        if let Some(resource) = self.resource.take() {
            let _ = self.return_tx.send(PoolReturn::Return(resource));
        }
        self.return_obligation.discharge();
    }
    
    /// Mark the resource as broken and discard it.
    /// 
    /// The pool will create a new resource to replace this one.
    pub fn discard(mut self) {
        self.resource.take();
        let _ = self.return_tx.send(PoolReturn::Discard);
        self.return_obligation.discharge();
    }
    
    /// How long this resource has been held.
    pub fn held_duration(&self) -> Duration {
        self.acquired_at.elapsed()
    }
}

impl<R> Deref for PooledResource<R> {
    type Target = R;
    fn deref(&self) -> &R { self.get() }
}

impl<R> DerefMut for PooledResource<R> {
    fn deref_mut(&mut self) -> &mut R { self.get_mut() }
}
